individualTax.notes: California imposes its own progressive individual income tax regime in addition to federal income tax.

corporateTax.notes: California generally taxes C corporations at 8.84% and also imposes an $800 minimum franchise tax in many cases.

salesTax.nexusRules: Businesses engaged in business in California generally collect the statewide rate plus any applicable district taxes.
salesTax.notes: Local district taxes can push combined rates above the statewide base.

propertyTax.notes: Property taxes are administered at the county level rather than by the FTB.

filingDeadline: LLC annual tax is generally due by the 15th day of the 4th month of the current tax year; business return due dates vary by entity classification.
overview: California is one of the hardest states to treat casually because the LLC annual tax, the income-based LLC fee, and the return itself are separate moving parts. A founder can be current on one of them and still be noncompliant on another.
practicalPointers: The fixed annual LLC tax is generally 800 USD.
California can impose a separate LLC fee based on California-source total income.
The annual tax, estimated fee, and return are related but not interchangeable.

sources.label: Applying tax to your sales and purchases

individualTax.notes: Texas does not impose a state individual income tax.

corporateTax.notes: Texas does not use a traditional corporate income tax. It generally imposes a franchise tax and annual information reporting obligations on taxable entities.

salesTax.nexusRules: Texas imposes a 6.25% state sales and use tax, and local jurisdictions can add up to 2% more for a maximum combined rate of 8.25%.
salesTax.notes: Remote sellers and in-state sellers should review permit, collection, and reporting rules on the Comptroller site.

propertyTax.notes: Property taxes are generally imposed and administered by local appraisal districts and taxing units.

filingDeadline: Annual franchise tax reports and related information reports are generally due May 15 each year.
overview: Texas is attractive because there is no individual income tax, but founders should not translate that into no compliance. Texas still runs a franchise-tax and information-reporting system that can create problems even when no tax is ultimately due.
practicalPointers: Franchise-tax reports are generally due May 15.
Entities at or below the no-tax-due threshold can still have information-report obligations.
Texas sales-tax nexus and Texas franchise-tax exposure are separate questions.

individualTax.notes: Delaware imposes its own individual income tax, but many TaxGuided readers care more about Delaware's entity-maintenance rules than its resident tax system.

corporateTax.notes: Delaware has a corporate income tax and a separate annual LLC tax regime that matters to formation-state comparisons.

salesTax.nexusRules: Delaware does not impose a state sales tax.
salesTax.notes: Delaware formation does not shield the business from other-state sales-tax nexus.

filingDeadline: Delaware LLC annual tax is generally due June 1 each year. LLCs do not file an annual report.
overview: Delaware is one of the most common U.S. formation states for foreign founders. The core operational rule is easy to miss: no LLC annual report, but a real annual tax due every year.
practicalPointers: The annual LLC tax is generally 300 USD.
Late payment generally adds a 200 USD penalty plus 1.5% monthly interest.

sources.label: Delaware annual report and tax information

individualTax.notes: Wyoming does not impose a state individual income tax.

corporateTax.notes: Wyoming does not impose a traditional state corporate income tax, which is one reason it appears so often in low-cost formation comparisons.

salesTax.nexusRules: Wyoming has a statewide sales and use tax and local additions can apply.
salesTax.notes: Formation in Wyoming does not prevent other states from asserting tax nexus where the business operates.

propertyTax.notes: Property taxes are handled through local systems.

filingDeadline: The annual report is due on or before the first day of the month of the entity's initial filing date, beginning with the first anniversary year.
overview: Wyoming is the low-cost benchmark state for many foreign-owned LLCs. The burden is light, but the anniversary-based annual-report deadline catches owners who assume every state uses a fixed calendar date.
practicalPointers: The minimum annual report fee is 60 USD.
The due date is anniversary-based rather than a single statewide filing date.
Reminder notices are generally sent by email.

sources.label: Wyoming The Choice is Yours publication

authority.name: Florida Department of State / Department of Revenue

individualTax.notes: Florida does not impose a state individual income tax.

corporateTax.notes: Florida imposes a corporate income/franchise tax on corporations and entities taxed as corporations. The current published rate is 5.5% for taxable years beginning on or after January 1, 2022.

salesTax.nexusRules: Florida's general state sales-tax rate is 6%, and discretionary surtax can apply locally.
salesTax.notes: Marketplace and remote-seller facts still need to be verified against Florida rules.

filingDeadline: Florida annual reports are due by May 1 each year. Separate tax-return deadlines can apply for corporations.
overview: Florida attracts founders with the no-personal-income-tax headline, but real compliance discipline still matters. The annual report is the state-maintenance issue most LLC owners cannot ignore.
practicalPointers: Florida LLC annual reports are due by May 1.
The on-time LLC annual report fee is 138.75 USD.
A 400 USD late fee generally applies after May 1 for LLCs that want to remain active.
